framework.primary: PyTorch (for NN) + scikit-learn (for trees) + NumPy/SciPy (for linear methods)
framework.reasoning: Paper does not specify framework. Finance/tabular ML canonical stack is PyTorch for NNs, scikit-learn for tree ensembles, and numpy/scipy + custom code for PCR/PLS/elastic net. No GPU strictly required — dataset fits in memory and NNs are shallow (≤5 layers, ≤32 neurons). CUDA beneficial but optional.

methods: StockReturnDataset.build_features(c_it, x_t) -> Z [NT x 920], R [NT x 1]

files.sir_mapping: Kronecker product feature construction z_it = x_t ⊗ c_it

methods: ElasticNetModel.tune(Z_train, R_train, Z_val, R_val, lambda_grid, rho_grid) -> best_params

files.sir_mapping: GLM with spline expansion and group lasso (Section 1.5)
files.note: Spline knot locations ASSUMED: equally spaced quantiles of training data (confidence 0.62)

RandomForestModel.fit(Z, R, L, m, B) -> None
NeuralNetModel.fit(Z_train, R_train, Z_val, R_val, config) -> None

LinearTrainer.fit_and_tune(model_class, Z_train, R_train, Z_val, R_val, param_grid) -> best_model
files.sir_mapping: Training pipeline Section 1.7: SGD, Adam, early stopping, batch norm, ensemble

tensor_flow.feature_construction:
FEATURE CONSTRUCTION:
  c_it: [N_t, 94] float32  ← raw stock characteristics
  x_t: [9] float32         ← macro predictors (8) + constant
  c_ranked = cross_sectional_rank(c_it)  → [N_t, 94] in [-1, 1]
  z_it = kronecker(x_t, c_ranked) + industry_dummies  → [N_t, 920]
  Z: [NT, 920] float32     ← full panel stacked
tensor_flow.nn_forward_pass:
NN FORWARD PASS (NN3 example):
  z: [B, 920] float32          ← input batch
  x0 = z  → [B, 920]
  x1 = ReLU(batch_norm(x0 @ W0 + b0))  → [B, 32]
  x2 = ReLU(batch_norm(x1 @ W1 + b1))  → [B, 16]
  x3 = ReLU(batch_norm(x2 @ W2 + b2))  → [B, 8]
  r_hat = x3 @ W3 + b3                  → [B, 1]
tensor_flow.pcr_pass:
PCR FORWARD:
  Z: [NT, 920] → SVD → Omega_K: [920, K]
  Z_reduced = Z @ Omega_K  → [NT, K]
  theta_K = OLS(Z_reduced, R)  → [K, 1]
  r_hat = Z_reduced @ theta_K → [NT, 1]
tensor_flow.pls_pass:
PLS FORWARD (SIMPLS):
  For j = 1..K:
    phi_j = Z.T @ R / ||Z.T @ R||  (univariate corr weights)
    w_j = Z.T @ R  (normalized)
    Z, R = orthogonalize w.r.t. previous components
  Omega_K: [920, K]; r_hat = (Z @ Omega_K) @ theta_K
tensor_flow.portfolio_construction:
PORTFOLIO:
  r_hat: [N_t] per stock  → sort → decile assignments → long top / short bottom
  bottom_up: sum(w_it * r_hat_it) for stocks i in portfolio p

model.variant: NN3  # one of: OLS, OLS3, ENet, PCR, PLS, GLM, RF, GBRT, NN1..NN5
model.n_ensemble_seeds: 10  # ASSUMED: paper states ensemble but not seed count (confidence 0.50)

entrypoints.description: Train a single model variant with annual refitting
Train all 13 model variants and save results table (replicates Table 1)
Evaluate saved models on test set; produce R², DM tests, portfolio Sharpe ratios
Build decile-sorted portfolios and compute Sharpe ratios (replicates Tables 7, 8)
Compute and plot variable importance across all models (replicates Figures 4, 5)

risk_assessment.description: CRSP and Compustat/characteristics data are proprietary and require subscription access. The 94-characteristic dataset from Green, Hand, Zhang (2017) is partially available but may require reconstruction.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Provide a synthetic data generator using random factor models. Include a download script stub with WRDS API instructions. Paper's characteristics list in Internet Appendix Table A.6 can guide reconstruction.

risk_assessment.description: Mini-batch size for SGD is not stated (confidence 0.45). Wrong batch size can significantly affect NN training dynamics and results.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Expose as config parameter with default 512. Add TODO comment. Ablation helper to test multiple sizes.

risk_assessment.description: Number of ensemble random seeds not stated (confidence 0.50). Affects variance of NN predictions.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default to 10 seeds. Abstract via config. Seeds loop is easily parallelized.

risk_assessment.description: Early stopping patience/criterion not specified (confidence 0.55). Different stopping rules can alter NN generalization markedly.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement patience-based stopping (default 5 epochs). Add TODO. Make patience a config param.

risk_assessment.description: Spline knot locations for GLM not specified (confidence 0.62). Affects GLM feature quality.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default to equally spaced quantiles of training distribution. Mark with TODO.

risk_assessment.description: L1 regularization grid for NN not specified (confidence 0.52). Paper uses L1 but not the tuning grid.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use log-spaced grid over [1e-4, 0.1]. Make grid a config parameter.

risk_assessment.description: Exact SIMPLS algorithm implementation for PLS may differ across libraries. Paper cites de Jong (1993).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use scikit-learn PLSRegression which implements NIPALS/SIMPLS. Validate against paper's K=3-6 component results.

risk_assessment.description: Annual refit protocol: training sample grows by 1 year at each refit over 30-year test period = 30 refits × 13 models = 390 training runs. Computationally intensive.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement recursive refitting loop with caching. Provide single-year evaluation mode for debugging.
